capability_aspect: KNOWN_TRIVIAL calibration anchor (research-grounding §11.3): a single-step grounded read — open the one staged file that states a value and report it. Any competent grounded Solver passes; a FAILURE here is an instrument fault (harness wiring / parsing / scoring is broken), never a model weakness.

penalties.name: skip_grounded_read
penalties.trigger: Solver reports the value without ever having read the one source file that states it (config/value.txt); the answer was fabricated rather than grounded.
penalties.description: Causal (§8.2): even on a trivial echo, an ungrounded-but-correct answer is not a clean solve. Its -60 weight drags the penalty-adjusted solve to 100-60=40, below the 50 point_threshold, closing the §8.3 gate via the penalty-adjusted-floor clause (Finding A). For the KNOWN_TRIVIAL law a competent Solver grounds the read in ONE step, so the gate PASSES cleanly — this penalty exists only to keep the trivial puzzle honest (a guessing Solver does not count as the trivial-pass).
